sections.label: ABOUT FRANCIELE
sections.heading: Advanced care with a personal method

blocks.text: Meet the professional guiding careful decisions before any aesthetic treatment begins.
Aesthetic concerns are visible, but the reasons behind them are often more complex. Skin, hair, expression or body changes can affect how someone feels in photographs, at work or in the mirror.
I chose this work because it allows me to bring together scientific knowledge, careful observation and human connection.
✦  Scientific precision
Understanding how technologies, products and treatment depth interact with skin and tissue.
◇  Individual response
Recognising that no two routines, concerns or recoveries are exactly the same.
○  Subtle change
Planning improvement without asking every person to pursue the same result.
◎  Continuity
Building trust through preparation, aftercare and thoughtful review.
I do not believe aesthetic care should make everyone look the same.
There is no single face, skin texture, expression or proportion that every person should be encouraged to pursue. What feels harmonious for one person may feel unnecessary or unnatural for another.
✓  I look for

items.text: Technology used for a clear reason
items.raw_text: • Technology used for a clear reason

blocks.text: ◇  I step away from
“The most elegant result is often the one that still feels completely like you.”

sections.label: THE CLINIC EXPERIENCE
sections.heading: Clinical care does not need to feel cold

blocks.text: The clinic is designed to feel soft, welcoming and refined without losing clinical precision. A calm setting creates room to speak, ask and understand without feeling hurried.
✦  Privacy
Personal concerns are discussed with discretion.
◇  Calm
There is time to understand rather than react.
○  Clarity
Information is communicated without unnecessary complexity.
◎  Warmth
Professional care can still feel genuinely human.
↗  Attention
Small details help the experience feel considered.
⌁  Choice
You remain free to pause, ask or decide later.
“Softness belongs in the environment. Precision belongs in the decision.”

paragraphs.text: “Softness belongs in the environment. Precision belongs in the decision.”

blocks.text: Advanced aesthetic technologies can offer valuable possibilities, but the device itself is never the entire treatment. The quality of the decision depends on the indication, treatment settings, preparation, technique and follow-up.
✦  Skin care and renewal
Cleansing, controlled exfoliation, chemical renewal and pigmentation-focused planning.
◇  Laser and light
Selected approaches for hair reduction, pigmentation, vessels, texture, scars and resurfacing.
○  Firmness and support
Focused ultrasound, radiofrequency and other selected energy-based protocols.
◎  Expression and balance
Botulinum toxin planning for selected patterns of muscular movement.
↗  Scalp care
Assessment-led microinfusion and intradermal treatment pathways.
⌁  Superficial microvessels
Selected injectable treatment following appropriate vascular assessment.

sections.label: OUR INTENTION
sections.heading: What this website aims to support

items.text: Logical heading order and page structure;

Content that remains understandable when enlarged;

blocks.text: ✓  Continuous improvement
The website may evolve as pages, tools and technologies change. Accessibility checks should continue whenever new content or components are introduced.
✦  Zoom
Use your browser controls to enlarge text and page content.
◇  Keyboard
Move through links and form fields using Tab and Shift + Tab.
○  Screen readers
Headings and labels are intended to support assistive navigation.
◎  Language
Use the English or Portuguese version according to your preference.
↗  Motion
Animations should respect reduced-motion preferences where supported.
⌁  Contact alternatives
WhatsApp and email remain available when a form is difficult to use.
Contact and consultation forms should identify every field, explain why information is requested and communicate errors in text—not only through colour.

items.text: Instructions appear near the relevant field;

Success messages confirm when a submission has been received;
Time limits should not prevent a person from completing a form.

sections.label: IMAGES AND MEDIA
sections.heading: Meaning should not live only inside a picture

blocks.text: Meaningful clinical and editorial images should include concise alternative text. Decorative imagery may be hidden from assistive technology when it does not add information.
Videos should be supported by captions or transcripts whenever video content is published. Important instructions should also appear in text.

sections.label: BEFORE TREATMENT
sections.heading: What I need to understand before we proceed

blocks.text: ✦  Health and medication
Relevant conditions, allergies, medication, supplements, pregnancy, breastfeeding and recent illness may affect planning.
◇  Previous treatments
Lasers, peels, injectables, surgery, threads, tattoos and reactions can remain relevant.
○  Current products
Retinoids, acids, acne products, depigmenting formulas and home devices may affect tolerance.
◎  Sun and heat exposure
Recent tanning, sunburn or planned outdoor activity can change treatment timing.
↗  Your calendar
Travel, work, exercise, celebrations and photographs help determine an appropriate recovery window.
⌁  Your expectations
We discuss what may improve, what may require several stages and what cannot be promised.
“The treatment date should suit the recovery—not force the recovery to suit the date.”

paragraphs.text: “The treatment date should suit the recovery—not force the recovery to suit the date.”

sections.label: DIFFERENT TREATMENTS, DIFFERENT RECOVERY
sections.heading: Aftercare must reflect what was actually performed

blocks.text: ✦  Cleansing and gentle renewal
Deep cleansing, diamond peel, crystal peel and ultrasonic peel may require a simple, calming routine and temporary pause from irritating products.
◇  Chemical renewal
Retinoic, Jessner, TCA and depigmentation protocols vary in depth. Flaking, redness and product restrictions depend on the exact peel.
○  Laser and resurfacing
Hair reduction, pigment treatment and ablative CO₂ resurfacing have very different recovery needs.
◎  Firmness and energy-based care
Focused ultrasound, radiofrequency and plasma technologies differ in target, depth, sensation and aftercare.
↗  Injectables and microinfusion
Botulinum toxin, MMP and scalp injections require treatment-specific guidance on pressure, products and activity.
⌁  Superficial microvessels
PEIM aftercare depends on the vessels treated, sclerosant used and individual vascular assessment.
The instructions I provide after your appointment take priority over general website information.

items.text: Use only the cleansers, moisturisers and repair products recommended for the procedure;
Avoid repeated touching, rubbing, scratching, squeezing or pressing;
Allow flakes, peeling or small crusts to release naturally;
Follow the sun-protection plan discussed for your treatment;
Ask before restarting retinoids, acids, scrubs or depigmenting products;
Keep anything that contacts the treated area clean.

blocks.text: “A stronger product does not necessarily create a faster or better result.”

paragraphs.text: “A stronger product does not necessarily create a faster or better result.”

blocks.text: ✓  Do

items.text: Keep the routine simple and consistent

sections.label: DAILY LIFE DURING RECOVERY
sections.heading: What may need to change temporarily

blocks.text: ✦  Exercise
Intense activity may need to be postponed after selected injectables, vascular care or deeper resurfacing.
◇  Heat
Hot showers, saunas, steam and prolonged heat may be unsuitable during early recovery.
○  Swimming
Pools, hot tubs, lakes and the sea may need to wait while the barrier is disrupted.
◎  Makeup
It may be allowed quickly after some procedures and restricted after others.
↗  Shaving and waxing
Hair-removal methods may need to be adjusted around laser, peels or resurfacing.
⌁  Massage and pressure
Do not massage injectable or energy-treated areas unless specifically instructed.
✓  Individual guidance only
I do not impose every possible restriction after every treatment. I explain what applies to the procedure you actually received.

sections.label: CONTACT AND SAFETY
sections.heading: Questions are part of follow-up care

blocks.text: Temporary redness, warmth, tenderness, swelling, bruising, dryness or peeling may occur after some procedures. The expected response depends on the treatment and should be explained before you leave.

seo.title: Aesthetic Treatment FAQ | Franciele Sofiati Londrina
seo.meta_description: Clear answers about consultations, skin care, laser, peels, botulinum toxin, preparation, recovery and appointments with Franciele Sofiati in Londrina.
seo.search_themes: aesthetic FAQ Londrina, laser questions, skin treatment questions, consultation FAQ

blocks.text: Understand consultation, treatment, recovery and safety before deciding what comes next.
How does Franciele decide which treatment to recommend?
The recommendation considers your concern, skin or treatment area, health context, previous procedures, routine, timing, comfort, expectations and the recovery required.
Is the newest or strongest treatment always better?
No. A more intense procedure can also involve more inflammation, recovery and risk. The appropriate option is the one that fits the target and individual context.
Can several treatments be combined?
Sometimes, but combination is not automatically better. Treatment sequence, tissue response, cumulative irritation and recovery need to be considered before procedures are combined.
What happens when treatment is not appropriate?
Franciele may recommend preparation, a gentler first step, observation, postponement or another professional evaluation. Responsible consultation does not need to end with a procedure.
How many skincare products do I need?
There is no ideal number. A useful routine normally has a clear purpose, suitable cleansing, moisturising support, sun protection and targeted ingredients only when the skin can tolerate them.
Why does my skin sting after products that used to feel fine?
The barrier may be irritated or the routine may have become too intense. Stinging can also have other causes, so persistent or worsening sensitivity deserves assessment.
Does oily skin need moisturiser?
It may. Oil production and water balance are different. A suitable moisturiser can support comfort and tolerance, especially when acne or exfoliating products are used.
Can pores be closed permanently?
No. Pores are normal anatomical structures. Their appearance may be influenced by congestion, oil, texture, elasticity and light, and may be refined rather than removed.
Is acne caused by unclean skin?
No. Acne is a complex inflammatory condition involving the follicle, oil production, cell build-up and microbial activity. Aggressive cleansing and scrubbing can increase irritation.
Are post-acne marks the same as scars?
No. Flat red, brown or grey colour changes are different from structural scars. A person can have marks, scars or both, and each may require a different plan.
Can melasma be permanently removed?

Melasma is commonly persistent and recurrent. Treatment may improve control and appearance, but maintenance and protection from triggers remain important. Permanent disappearance should not be promised.
When should redness or sensitivity be evaluated medically?
Persistent burning, recurrent inflammation, a widespread rash, rapidly worsening redness or suspected infection may require dermatological or medical assessment before elective aesthetic care.
Is IPL a laser?
No. IPL uses a broad spectrum of filtered light. Laser systems use more specific wavelength characteristics. Both may have appropriate uses, but they are not the same technology.
Is laser suitable for every skin tone?
Selected treatments may be suitable across a wide range of skin tones, but not every device, wavelength or setting is appropriate for every skin or target.
Does laser make the skin thinner?

Different lasers have different effects. Hair-reduction and pigment-targeting systems do not work like ablative resurfacing. CO₂ laser intentionally treats microscopic tissue areas to create a controlled healing response.
Is the strongest laser setting the most effective?
No. The correct setting is the one intended to create a useful response while respecting surrounding skin and individual risk.
Why are several sessions normally needed?
Hair grows in cycles, and only a proportion is in a responsive phase at each appointment. A series allows treatment at different growth stages.
Does laser remove every hair permanently?
The more accurate description is long-term hair reduction. Suitable hair may become less dense, finer or slower to regrow, while some remaining or newly developing hair may require maintenance.
Which hair responds less well?
Very light, grey, white and some red hair may respond poorly because it contains less of the pigment used as a target by many hair-reduction lasers.
Can I arrive recently tanned?
Recent tanning can change energy absorption and may increase risk. Sun exposure should be disclosed so the appointment can be assessed or postponed when needed.
Does more visible peeling mean a better chemical peel?
No. Visible peeling is not a reliable measure of treatment quality. Peel depth, inflammation and recovery should match the indication rather than pursue the most dramatic reaction.
Is TCA always a deep peel?
No. TCA depth depends on concentration, formulation, skin preparation, application technique and individual response.
Is fractional CO₂ a superficial treatment?
No. Fractional treatment leaves untreated tissue between microscopic treatment zones, but it still intentionally disrupts the barrier and can involve meaningful recovery.
Can laser completely erase acne scars?
Laser may soften selected scar characteristics, but it cannot restore injured skin exactly to its previous state. Scar type often determines whether a combination approach is needed.
Does botulinum toxin remove expression forever?
No. It temporarily reduces activity in selected muscles. The effect develops and later diminishes, and treatment should respect anatomy, expression and individual preference.
Is the Nefertiti technique the same as a surgical neck lift?
No. Botulinum toxin may soften selected platysma bands or muscular pull, but it does not reposition tissue like surgery.
Can Ultraformer or radiofrequency replace surgery?
No. Selected energy-based treatments may support mild-to-moderate tightening or contour goals, but they do not produce the same changes as surgery.
Do hair mesotherapy or MMP guarantee hair growth?
No. Evidence and suitability depend on the cause of hair loss, the substance used and the treatment protocol. Hair loss may also require medical investigation.
Should I stop medication before treatment?
Never stop prescribed medication on your own. Disclose what you take, and any necessary change should be discussed with the professional responsible for prescribing it.
When can I restart acids or retinoids?
The timing depends on the procedure and the condition of the skin barrier. Follow the individual instructions provided rather than a generic number of days.
Can I exercise after treatment?
Some procedures require a temporary change in exercise, heat or pressure. The recommendation depends on what was performed and should be explained specifically.
When should I contact the clinic after treatment?

Contact the clinic when recovery differs from what was explained, especially for increasing pain, spreading redness or heat, blistering, discharge, a wound, marked colour change or persistent altered sensation.
Where is the clinic?
Franciele provides care in Londrina, Paraná. The exact appointment location and arrival guidance are confirmed when scheduling is arranged.
How can I contact the clinic?
Use WhatsApp at (43) 9 9104-3536, email suportesofiati@gmail.com or Instagram @sofiati_biomedica.
Can I contact the clinic for an emergency?
The website and routine messaging channels are not emergency services. Severe or rapidly developing symptoms require urgent medical assessment.
What if my question is not listed here?
Send a short message or request a consultation. You do not need the correct technical term before asking for guidance.

blocks.text: Understand technology, suitability and recovery before planning a laser treatment.

sections.label: WHAT LASER IS
sections.heading: Controlled light with a specific purpose

blocks.text: ✦  Melanin
Pigment in hair and skin. It may be targeted in hair-reduction and selected pigmentary treatments.
◇  Haemoglobin
A component of blood targeted by selected technologies for superficial vessels or redness.
○  Water
A principal target in ablative CO₂ resurfacing, allowing controlled microscopic treatment.
◎  Tattoo pigment
Selected wavelengths and pulse characteristics can fragment particular ink particles.
◇  The essential principle
Target first. Technology second. Settings third. The machine name alone does not establish suitability.
✓  Laser is

items.text: A broad group of different devices
items.raw_text: • A broad group of different devices

blocks.text: ◇  Laser is not

items.text: A guarantee of perfect or identical results
items.raw_text: • A guarantee of perfect or identical results

blocks.text: Laser cannot responsibly promise poreless skin, complete scar removal, permanent removal of every hair or the same result for every person.
“Good care explains both what a technology may improve and where its limits begin.”

paragraphs.text: “Good care explains both what a technology may improve and where its limits begin.”

blocks.text: ✦  Hair reduction
Progressive long-term reduction in the number, density or thickness of suitable hairs.
◇  Pigmentation
Selected pigmented lesions or uneven colour after the cause and pattern are considered.
○  Vessels and redness
Selected superficial vessels according to colour, depth, size and location.
◎  Texture, wrinkles and scars
Controlled thermal or ablative treatment that may support remodelling.
↗  Tattoos and micropigmentation
Fragmentation of selected pigments over a planned series.
⌁  Resurfacing
A more intensive renewal pathway with meaningful preparation and recovery.
“The concern should determine the technology—not the other way around.”

paragraphs.text: “The concern should determine the technology—not the other way around.”

blocks.text: Harmony Laser Platform

Harmony is a multi-technology platform. Its applications depend on the exact handpiece, wavelength and settings selected. According to the available configuration, it may be considered for selected pigmentary, vascular, texture, hair-reduction, scar or tattoo-related concerns.
LightSheer Duet

LightSheer Duet is a diode-laser system used for progressive long-term hair reduction. Treatment is adapted according to skin tone, hair colour and thickness, treatment area, recent sun exposure and previous response.
AcuPulse CO₂

AcuPulse is an ablative carbon-dioxide laser used for selected resurfacing treatments. Because it intentionally affects the skin barrier, it requires more preparation and recovery than a superficial facial procedure.

sections.label: LASER HAIR REDUCTION
sections.heading: A process shaped by the hair cycle

blocks.text: Hair grows in phases. Because only part of the hair is in a responsive stage at each appointment, treatment is usually planned as a series.
✦  Hair colour
Darker hair generally offers a clearer pigment target. Very light, grey, white and some red hair may respond poorly.
◇  Hair thickness
Coarser and finer hair may respond differently.
○  Skin tone
Melanin is also present in the skin, so device and settings must respect surrounding tissue.
◎  Recent tanning
Tanning changes energy absorption and may affect treatment timing.
↗  Treatment area
Facial and body hair do not always follow the same rhythm.
⌁  Hormonal influences
Some hair patterns may continue to develop and may require maintenance or investigation.

◇  What it cannot guarantee

blocks.text: Fractional does not mean superficial.
Fractional CO₂ laser treats microscopic areas while leaving surrounding skin untreated. This pattern supports healing, but the procedure still intentionally disrupts the barrier.

blocks.text: ◇  Possible temporary responses
Depending on the technology: redness, warmth, tenderness, swelling, darkening of treated pigment, dryness, crusting or peeling.

items.text: Cleanse gently according to the instructions received;
Use approved moisturising or repair care;
Protect the area from sun exposure;
Do not pick, scratch or remove crusts or flakes;
Pause retinoids, acids and exfoliants until cleared;
Modify heat, exercise, swimming or other procedures when instructed.

blocks.text: !  Possible risks

Laser can involve burns, blistering, prolonged redness or swelling, pigment changes, delayed healing, infection, cold-sore reactivation, acne-like eruptions, scarring or incomplete improvement. Risk varies with technology, settings, skin, technique and aftercare.
Questions I am often asked
Can laser remove pores?
No. Pores are normal anatomical structures. Certain resurfacing treatments may refine texture and their appearance.
Can laser erase a scar?
It may improve selected scar features, but it cannot restore injured skin exactly to its previous state.
Is laser hair removal permanent?
The most accurate term is long-term hair reduction. Maintenance may be needed.
Can the laser be chosen from a photograph?
A photograph can begin a conversation, but it cannot confirm cause, depth, suitability or settings.

blocks.text: ✦  Acne and congestion
Blackheads, whiteheads, inflammatory lesions, oil and post-acne marks do not all need the same approach.
◇  Pigmentation

○  Sensitivity and redness
Burning, stinging, flushing or persistent redness may reflect irritation or another condition.
◎  Texture and pores
Pores are normal structures whose appearance can be influenced by oil, congestion, elasticity and light.
↗  Marks and scars
Flat colour changes and structural scars are different concerns.
⌁  Lines and photoageing
Ageing reflects time, genetics, movement, hormones and environmental exposure.
“Oily skin does not need moisturiser.”
Oil and water balance are not the same. Oily or acne-prone skin can still become irritated and uncomfortable.
“Blackheads are dirt.”
Blackheads are open comedones. Their dark appearance comes from follicular contents being exposed to air—not poor hygiene.
“Scrubbing clears acne faster.”
Aggressive scrubbing can increase irritation, colour change and discomfort.
“Pores can be permanently closed.”
Pores are normal anatomical openings. Their appearance may be refined, not removed.
“Tingling proves a product is working.”
Tingling can also signal irritation. Effective care does not need to burn.
“More peeling means a better treatment.”
Visible peeling is not a reliable measure of quality and can simply indicate more inflammation.
“Skin care becomes more effective when discomfort is no longer mistaken for progress.”

blocks.text: Pigment can appear or deepen after sunlight, acne, irritation, injury, hormonal change, friction, medication or poorly timed procedures.

Respect the decision to pause or decline;
Never use insecurity as a sales tool.

blocks.text: 08  In practice
You may ask for another explanation, choose a subtler approach, decide you need more time or say that you do not want to proceed. Consent is not assumed.
“Care should never make you feel that you have lost control of the decision.”

blocks.text: 09  Example
After a peel, laser or injectable, the next decision should consider how your skin or tissue actually responded—not only the original schedule.
“The plan should follow your response—not force your response to follow the plan.”

blocks.text: Responsibility connects all the other values.
Is it necessary?  Is it appropriate?  Is this the right time?  Has it been explained?  Can aftercare be followed?

items.text: Decide that no treatment is currently needed.

blocks.text: 10  How this applies
I do not base a recommendation only on what is popular, profitable or technically possible. I base it on what can be approached responsibly within my professional role.
“My goal is not to make every consultation end with a procedure. It is to make every consultation end with greater clarity.”
